California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 15, 1990
vash webster
Val,ley Insurance Program
448 west Sha!, Avenue
Fresno, CA 937 04
Dear Mr. Webster:
This is in response to your Juty 6, 1990, letter requesting
advice concerning the conflict of interest provisions of the
PoliticaL Reform Act (the rra66n1.1/
I SSUES
You have sought our advice regarding the following objections
to f j.Iing under your agency's conflict of interest code:
1. 
John D. Hynes, resident agent for Giddings, Corby,
Hynes, Inc., ('rccHt'), has expressed !'is opilion that he should not
bE designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. 
Mr'
Eynes siates that he is rt.. not a Director, officer, Partner,
Tiustee, Employee, or in any lray responsible for management for
Giddings, Corby, Hynes, the current resident agent, I assune no
statenent of econornic interest would be applicable. ..rr'
2. 
Jin L. Peterson, certified public accountant for Martin
Linger & co., has also expressed his opinion that he should not be
deslgnated in your agency's confLict of interest code' !'!r '
pet"iso., has slated tnat-he rr... does not nake any decisions which
nay effect (sic) financial interests. rr
1/ 
Government code sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
co.mi=slo., regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18OOO; et seq. iil references to regulations are to ritle
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
Re:
Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-455
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 80? o Sacrarnento CA 958O4.O8O7 . (916)322'5660


Fi■e No. A-90-455
Page 2
Accordingly, Mr. Peterson has recommended that he be excluded
from the Form 73o filing requirements as Auditor of the vaIley
Insurance Program Joint Powers Agency.
CONCI,USIONS
1. 
The position of rrresident agentr is specifically
designated in the code for the val1ey Insurance Program JPA and
therifore persons in that capacity are required to file statements
of economic interests.
Itlr. Hynes, as a resident agent, is required to disclose his
personal interests as set out in the disclosure category portion
of tne Val1ey fnsurance Progran's conflict of interest code. IIr.
-tlynes does not disclose those interests held by his ernployer.
2. 
The position of "auditorrr is also at present
specifically designated in your conflict of interest code. All
plrsons occupying that position are subject to the financial
discLosure provisions of the code.
FACTS
The conflict of interest code for the Va11ey Insurance
Program's Joint Powers Agency was subuitted to the Fair Political
Praitices Corunj.ssion (the rrcommissionrr) for review and approval.
The commission approved your agency's conflict of interest code on
June 11, 1990. Your code became effective July 11, 1990, and
initial 
statements were due no later than Augnrst 13, 1990.
DT SCUSSION
When promulgating a conflict of interest code, nulti-county
jurisdictional 
agencies such as yours, are reguired to follow the
procedures as set out in Comrnission regulation 18750.1 (copy
enclosed). Regulation 18750.1 specifically requires that each
agency which adopts or arnends a conflict of interest code must
utilize a 45-day public comment period. Additionally, all
designated positions are to be sent a copy of the notice and the
proposed conflict of interest code 45 days prior to the adoption
of the code. The purpose of this direct notice is to ensure that
all persons affected by the proposed code have been given adequate
notice and a fair opportunity to present their views. In your
June 29, 1990, letter you stated that the agency follolred this
notice procedure.


FiIe No. A-90-4 55
Page 3
The concerns now being expressed by the resident agent and
the auditor should have been raised and resol,ved during the code
adoption process. Unfortunately, these cornments were not received
during the comment period and thus the code was adopted by the
Valley Insurance Program and approved by the Fair Political
Practj,ces Cornrnission. These positions are presently covered by
the code and must file initial 
statements no later than August 13,
1990.
cov. code Section 87306 provides that every agency shall
arnend its code when change is necessitated by changed
circunstances. The vall-ey Insurance Program can take steps to
amend its code to delete positions if those Positions were
included erroneously. Any anendnents to the code nust be carried
out under the procedures described in Commission Regulation
18750.l, (copy enclosed).
I believe a more important question here is whether these
positions shoul.d be deleted.
Conflict of interest codes must specifically enumerate
positions wj,thin the agency which involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
Lave a lnaterial financial effect on any financial interest for
each such enumerated position, the specific types of investments,
business positions, interests in real property, and sources.of
income which are reportable. An investment, business position,
interest in real property, or source of income shall be made
reportable by the conflict of Interest Code if the business entity
in which the investnent or busj,ness position is held, the interest
in real property, or the income or source of incorne rnay
foreseeably be affected materially by any decision nade or
participated in by the designated enployee by virtue of his or her
pos ition .
By naking a governmental decision we mean a public officiai-2/
rrmakes a governmental decisionrr lrhen he or she, acting within the
authority of his or her office:
(1) votes on a matteri
(2) Appoints a personi
(3) obligates or cornmits his or her agency
course of action;
to any
on behalf of
(4) Enters into any contractual agreement
his or her agency i
2/  The
officer′
agency..
de f init ion
employee
. (Section
of a pub■ic offic■a■ means
or consu■tant of a state or
82048).
every menber,
loca1 governnent


Fi■e No. A-90-455
Page 4
(5) Determines not to act, $iithin the meaning of
sub-paragraphs (1.) , (2) , (3) and (4), unless such
detennination is made because of his or her
financial interest.
Regulation l-8700 (b)
A public official 
"participates in the making of a
governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of
his or her position, he or she:
(1) Negotiates, without significant substantive review,
srith a governmental entity or private person
regarding the decision; or
(2) Advises or makes recornrnendations to the decision-
naker, either directly or without significant
intervening substantive rev j.ew, by:
(A) conducting research or making any
investigation whj.ch requires the exercise of
j udgrnent on the part of the official- and the
purpose of which is to inftuence the decision; or
(B) Preparing or presentj.ng any report, analysis
or opinion, oralLy or in writing, which requires
the exercise of j udgrrnent on the part of the
official 
and the Purpose of vrhich is to influence
the decision.
Regulation 18700 (c)
Page 7 of the Joint Powers Agreenent provides in part.
that the auditor tr... shal1 pay denands against the authority
r^rhen the demands have been approved by the Executive
Cornmittee.rr The agreement further provides that "...the
auditor sha11 rnake, or contract with " a certified Public
Accountant to make an annual audit...rr. 
Initially 
it appears
that the auditor's function may be ministerial in nature as
its paying demands under the direction of the Executive
conmitlee. If, hoerever, the auditor does have the authority
to contract on behalf of the Authority, it would not be
appropriate to delete this position from the code.
As to the resident agent, if he or she does not nake or
participate in the making of decisions as described above,
including naking recommendations as described above by
negulation 18700(c), it would be appropriate to propose.this
position for deLetion' Should you propose to delete this
|osition, it t/i1l be necessary for you to provide a detail'ed
job description or a duty statement.


If you shou■d have further questions or concerns regarding
this ■etter′  or shou■d you need assistance in the amendment
process incorporating the above changes′
 please do nOt hesitate to
contact the Legal Division at (9■
6)322-590■
.
Fi■e No. A-90-455
Page 5

Encl osures
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting General Counsel

sections.question:
You have sought our advice regarding the following objections to filing under your agency's conflict of interest code:

1. John D. Hynes, resident agent for Giddings, Corby, Hynes, Inc., has expressed his opinion that he should not be designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. Mr. Hynes states that he is not a Director, officer, Partner, Trustee, Employee, or in any way responsible for management for Giddings, Corby, Hynes, the current resident agent, I assume no statement of economic interest would be applicable.

2. Jim L. Peterson, certified public accountant for Martin Linger & Co., has also expressed his opinion that he should not be designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. Mr. Peterson has stated that he does not make any decisions which may effect financial interests. Accordingly, Mr. Peterson has recommended that he be excluded from the Form 730 filing requirements as Auditor of the Valley Insurance Program Joint Powers Agency.
sections.conclusion:
1. The position of 'resident agent' is specifically designated in the code for the Valley Insurance Program JPA and therefore persons in that capacity are required to file statements of economic interests. Mr. Hynes, as a resident agent, is required to disclose his personal interests as set out in the disclosure category portion of the Valley Insurance Program's conflict of interest code. Mr. Hynes does not disclose those interests held by his employer.

2. The position of 'auditor' is also at present specifically designated in your conflict of interest code. All persons occupying that position are subject to the financial disclosure provisions of the code.

sections.question_synthetic: Should the positions of resident agent (John D. Hynes) and auditor (Jim L. Peterson) be excluded from the Valley Insurance Program's conflict of interest code filing requirements based on their contentions that they do not make or participate in governmental decisions affecting financial interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both the resident agent and auditor positions are currently designated in the Valley Insurance Program's conflict of interest code and are therefore required to file statements of economic interests. However, the FPPC acknowledges that these positions could be amended and deleted from the code if they do not actually involve making or participating in governmental decisions, requiring detailed job descriptions or duty statements for such amendments.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: High quality extraction from well-formatted FPPC advice letter. Document contains clear ISSUES/CONCLUSIONS sections with technical regulatory discussion. Minor OCR errors noted in original text (e.g., 'rccHt' appears to be 'GCH', 'effect' spelled 'eeffect') but did not impede extraction. File number A-90-455 confirmed throughout document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have sought our advice regarding the following objections to filing under your agency's conflict of interest code:

1. John D. Hynes, resident agent for Giddings, Corby, Hynes, Inc., has expressed his opinion that he should not be designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. Mr. Hynes states that he is not a Director, officer, Partner, Trustee, Employee, or in any way responsible for management for Giddings, Corby, Hynes, the current resident agent, I assume no statement of economic interest would be applicable.

2. Jim L. Peterson, certified public accountant for Martin Linger & Co., has also expressed his opinion that he should not be designated in your agency's conflict of interest code. Mr. Peterson has stated that he does not make any decisions which may effect financial interests. Accordingly, Mr. Peterson has recommended that he be excluded from the Form 730 filing requirements as Auditor of the Valley Insurance Program Joint Powers Agency.

CONCLUSION: 1. The position of 'resident agent' is specifically designated in the code for the Valley Insurance Program JPA and therefore persons in that capacity are required to file statements of economic interests. Mr. Hynes, as a resident agent, is required to disclose his personal interests as set out in the disclosure category portion of the Valley Insurance Program's conflict of interest code. Mr. Hynes does not disclose those interests held by his employer.

2. The position of 'auditor' is also at present specifically designated in your conflict of interest code. All persons occupying that position are subject to the financial disclosure provisions of the code.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Valley Insurance Program that a resident agent and auditor have objected to filing conflict of interest disclosures, but their positions are designated in the approved code and therefore required to file. The FPPC indicates these positions could potentially be deleted through proper amendment procedures if they can be shown not to involve decision-making authority.
